concept.title: Understanding Patterns
concept.shortdesc: Patterns express observable conditions that generate evidence for planning.

conbody.p: A pattern is a declarative rule with fields such as id, applies_to, signals, and asserts. Pattern evaluation emits evidence when artifact signals match pattern requirements.

ul.li: signals: deterministic conditions such as filename or root element checks.
asserts: role and confidence emitted into evidence.

section.title: Fallback patterns
section.p: Fallback patterns are classification helpers and are evaluated only when semantic matches are absent and fallback mode is enabled. This keeps classification deterministic while avoiding over-assertion.

section.title: How planning uses patterns
section.p: Planning does not run discovery again. It reads evidence forwarded through artifact metadata and emits action templates from plugin emit_actions(...) methods.
